review_title: Reliable and User-Friendly Network Monitoring with Strong Performance Insights
description: My overall experience with ManageEngine OpManager has been very positive, as it provides a reliable and efficient way to monitor and manage network infrastructure across multiple environments. The tool offers strong visibility into device performance, easy troubleshooting through alerts and dashboards, and supports a wide range of devices in multi-vendor setups. While the initial setup and some advanced configurations can be a bit complex, once implemented, it significantly improves network uptime, reduces manual effort, and delivers good value for money for day-to-day operations.
author: Atul S.
pros: What I like most about ManageEngine OpManager is its easy deployment and user-friendly interface, along with powerful SNMP-based monitoring that provides real-time visibility into network devices like routers, switches, firewalls, and servers. The tool offers excellent device discovery, customizable dashboards, and reliable alerts, which help in quick troubleshooting and reducing downtime, while also delivering detailed performance metrics and strong value for money for enterprise environments.
cons: What I like least about ManageEngine OpManager is that the initial setup and advanced configuration can be complex, especially in large environments, and the user interface feels somewhat outdated compared to newer tools. Additionally, some advanced features require extra licensing, reporting customization is limited in certain cases, and performance may slow down when managing a large number of devices, with support response times occasionally being slower than expected.

review_title: Efficient and user-friendly network monitoring solution
description: Overall, the experience has been good. It helps us monitor network performance, reduce downtime, and quickly identify issues.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: OpManager gives good visibility of network devices, servers, and performance from a single dashboard. Monitoring and alerts are easy to configure.
cons: Some advanced features take a little time to explore initially, but overall the tool works smoothly for daily monitoring tasks.

review_title: Mixed. Some good points, some areas for improvement
author: Timothy O.
pros: - The number of types of events and conditions that can be monitored is very extensive - Large amount of data ingested can be very useful in troubleshooting ongoing issues - Performance is generally very smooth - A lot of active development meaning new features and security flaws are regularly fixed
cons: - Catch 22 with the conditions and data means that while out of the box it does work, to get the most of out the solution requires an extensive amount of time invested to customize the monitoring to tailor it to your needs and filter out noise - Interface is somewhat dated and noisy and very confused in its design. Some elements are clean and quick to interpret while others can be overly noisy and present to much information at that level when it would be better suited further in - Cost can be quite expensive

review_title: OpManager is a winner in the System Admin space
description: Overall, I am very happy with how ManageEngine OpManager makes my job easier, it gives a single pane of glass for monitoring and detecting outages. It increases the IT Departments productivity by being able to react and respond quicker to and detected issues.
author: Devon H.
pros: The ease of deployment was a big perk of OpManager, the agents are easy to group and deploy based on Organization Departments or other identifiers like system name, or other system values. The UI is very intuitive and easy to navigate, with custom dashboards being one of my favourite features.
cons: If I had to choose one con, it would be the on-going maintenance of detected systems where agents are or previously were installed, specifically in the case where a system has been renamed, it doesn't always clean up the old record in a timely manner, leaving duplicate entries for the same system.

review_title: Is OpManager for you?
description: It has been a good experience. It takes a little bit of time to get things fully configured and get rid of the false negative monitoring. It is definitely easier to manage and a better value than our previous PRTG deployment.
author: Joel B.
pros: Ease of use and setup. Set up a dashboard and map without much effort. I like being able to view servers by application and being able to get more info on the apps. Agent is not required.
cons: Status reporting is mixed. Servers show needs attention because manufacture name changed on VM. Basic login and SNMP discovery would not resolve the issue. The only real fix I found was to deploy the agent. Security scoring is a bit confusing. I would like to be able to see what is missing from the scoring to be able to improve the overall score.

review_title: Best Monitoring Tool
description: ManageEngine OpManager is a powerful secure and reasonably priced network monitoring software that offers centralized visibility, dependable alerts, and comprehensive reporting for servers, virtual infrastructure, and devices. The overall value, ease of monitoring, and performance insights make it a good option, especially for businesses looking to minimize downtime and control network health in a single dashboard, even though the initial setup and configuration may take some time.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: With OpManager the tracking of IT asset like severs, switches, routers, firewalls is made easy together with virtualized infrastructure in real time providing a high level of security and also provide alerts well on time no need to make server morning health checks manually.
cons: device discovery and setup through SNMP can be challenging and some users find configuration and custom reporting harder but for me it was not much since i have been using some of ManageEngine products like ServiceDesk plus and password manager
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Powerful Network Monitoring with a Learning Curve
description: My overall experience with ManageEngine OpManager has been very positive, especially for day-to-day network monitoring and management.
author: Don M.
pros: It provides real-time visibility into the entire IT infrastructure—including servers, routers, switches, and virtual environments—through a single dashboard. This makes it easy to track performance, detect issues early, and reduce downtime.
cons: One of the main drawbacks of ManageEngine OpManager is its complex setup and configuration, especially for large or enterprise environments. Initial deployment and fine-tuning of alerts, thresholds, and templates can be time-consuming and require experience.

review_title: Great opportunity to use Opamn
description: ManageEngine OpManager is generally praised for its comprehensive network monitoring, user-friendly interface, and robust features that help IT teams efficiently manage and troubleshoot network infrastructure.
author: Arjun R.
pros: I appreciate ManageEngine OpManager's user-friendly interface, comprehensive network monitoring, real-time alerts, and customizable dashboards, which simplify network management and enhance efficiency.
cons: I found the user interface of ManageEngine OpManager to be somewhat cluttered and not very intuitive, making navigation challenging at times.

review_title: Great UI for all.
description: Fast rollout with easy management. A good support team with the chat option gives quick and clear answers when production systems are impacted.
author: Shawn B.
pros: Great UI, with easy management options and functionality. The ability to import large numbers of devices with CSV file and discovery to give quick up down status for all new devices.
cons: Devices that must be tied to hardware templates for password validation and error reporting. WMI or CLI should just import all the details.

review_title: Great appliance for Network monitoring, With great customer service
description: What I liked most about OpManager was its user-friendly interface and real-time monitoring, which made it easy to track network performance and quickly respond to issues.
author: Kimberly S.
pros: What I liked most about OpManager was its user-friendly interface and real-time monitoring, which made it easy to track network performance and quickly respond to issues.
cons: I did not encounter any significant issues while using this application. The application met my expectations with no noticeable shortcomings

review_title: Effective tool for centralized network visibility
description: my overall experience with op manager is really fantastic, it enables centralized monitoring of devices, allow me to check their health status in real time, provides email notifications for alarms and offers detailed reports to review device performance over any required time period
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Op manager is an effective tool for centrally monitoring our organizational network devices and servers. since selecting op manager , the challenge of monitoring the devices from a single, centralized plateform has been significantly reduced.
cons: some tasks in op manager involve multiple navigation steps , and new user may require training due to product's complexity.

review_title: Great experience!!
description: Great experience, my ManageEngine products are top notch! What more could I ask for? The price is reasonable considering the possibilities.
author: Christophe R.
pros: Many different types of probes for monitoring servers, easy to install, fully customizable, easy asset management
cons: The interface is so comprehensive that it is sometimes difficult to determine where to click to customize everything.

review_title: product is great
description: all good, since I started working on this I have learned a lot and now I can configure servers easily.
author: Jorge O.
pros: easy to manage, and configuration is straight forward. we also get a lot of material to easy follow instructions
cons: difficult to upgrade, especially when we are three versions down, but it is acceptable. patches are applied easily

review_title: NCM Opmanager review
author: Pavan B.
pros: All‑in‑one visibility via real‑time, customizable dashboards Automated discovery & topology mapping for rapid setup Granular alerting so you only get notified on critical issues Built‑in reporting & SLA tools for easy compliance and planning Modular, scalable licensing that grows with your environment
cons: User Interface feels dated and can be less intuitive compared to newer tools Alert storms when thresholds aren’t finely tuned, leading to noise Custom reporting is limited, sometimes forcing workarounds or exports to Excel Performance can degrade at very large scales without substantial hardware Modular licensing complexity—adding server/app or virtualization packs drives up cost Support response times vary, especially for lower‑tier plans

review_title: Good Product for Monitoring
description: I feel this is the best tool as worked till date, worked with other tools as Moogsoft, Solar winds didnt find this Good
author: Mohammed H.
pros: OpManager is user friendly any one any background can understand if you leave them for 10 mins to manage the application
cons: we see the auto discovery of the VMs is happeneing and all the VMs being monitored without adding them manually

review_title: Seamless Monitoring with OPM
description: A very good experience among from other monitoring tools with lots of options for forecasting and reports
author: Dipak S.
pros: Covered each aspect in monitoring with lots of available options gives depths analysis and root cause
cons: More customisation should be their for suppressing alerts in specific time period, threshold values could be customised better.

review_title: OPM for easy infra monitoring
description: Good to use for small scale company, cost effective product, easy plug and play use. Lots of articles for self learn and use.
author: Ganesh B.
pros: Easily monitor N number of various flavour device linux, windows , storage in single dashboard view.
cons: Nothing on cons, all basic features will used. Some device MIB values are not available. So not able to add some old storage

review_title: A Robust Network Monitor for Traditional IT Infrastructures
description: OpManager effectively monitors a wide range of devices—servers, routers, switches, firewalls, virtual machines, etc.—with real-time performance metrics.
author: Islam A.
pros: It supports a wide range of devices (routers, switches, firewalls, servers, VMs, etc.)., Custom widgets, topology maps, and business views make it easy to tailor the monitoring experience to specific needs.
cons: Complex Initial Setup for Larger Environments, There are many nested menus and sections, which can hinder quick navigation until you're familiar with the platform.

review_title: Track dependencies between virtual and physical layers properly
author: Hassan M.
pros: The ability of OpManager to monitor physical as well as virtualized environments at once was particularly valuable in my use. Monitoring VM performance metrics in alignment with the host system stats is a big plus that contributed to better management of hybrid systems.
cons: Functional behavior I found was inconvenient was that when adding very large vmware clusters, it sometimes required further configuration to get performance counters perfectly synched across each node.

review_title: Network management
description: Good product having everything under one product. We can monitor the network uptime downtime. Alerts
author: Nilam T.
pros: AI and integration also discovery of network devices and alerts mechanism. Scope of devices and security
cons: Timely updates to customer regarding the new upgraded and features upgrated by zoho team it's helps customer to upgrade their products

review_title: Review of OpManager
description: It's been good over the last 10 years of use here and at my previous employer. It was a large enterprise and this is a SMB.
author: Jeromey L.
pros: Versatility and features. OpManager covers all our monitoring needs from network to servers. The map feature assists in troubleshooting and visibility by system.
cons: ManageEngine will change things with the interface without notice to customers. This causes issues for staff.

review_title: Beneficial in adding value to my working day
author: Adiel O.
pros: The bandwidth utilization report in OpManager helped me to assess the bandwidth utilization of Application on core links. Before the project, there were many patterns that were not easily noticeable and when I used NetFlow data, I was able to enforce some traffic policies to enhance performance when busy.
cons: There are occasions where the reporting engine is rigid in presenting historical bandwidth reports as per the selected date period. It required exporting the raw data into a spreadsheet for generating the trend reports beyond the static time intervals.

review_title: Ensured Network uptime and availability of solutions in case of a problem
author: Tiffany V.
pros: Bandwidth utilization and network traffic data flow with OpManager which helps to find out which circuits consume more bandwidth. This traffic data has been important for enhancing the performance of the network and it would not have been possible to provide detailed reports based on it.
cons: One of the problems I discovered with this software is that of the device discovery feature. For most devices it is well organized, but some of the recent or specific devices may need some configuration to be noticed.

review_title: Helpful in the management of our large communication network
author: Faisal M.
pros: The capacity of OpManager to oversee servers, storage units and other devices made it easy do get full network oversight. The dynamic and flexible dashboards and the detailed reports were useful for making efficient decisions and anticipating maintenance works hence reducing downtimes.​
cons: In the beginning, it was necessary to add some old-aged devices into the system, which was quite a long process. There is a possibility of integration of this process for improving the experience of users.​

review_title: Quickly increased my network uptime figures
description: In my day to day working activities, OpManager offered a broader view of the network traffic patterns and the distribution of services offered in virtualized/duplicate and physical server systems. On the proactive alerting we have been able to minimize on more link downtime incidents.
author: Hassene J.
pros: The one which I found most useful is the performance monitoring of the virtual devices. In my case I utilized it in association with a VMware environment, and was impressed with its ability to identify hosts and VMs while monitoring the CPU, memory and disk usage among others.
cons: Delay was observed in NetFlow configuration process. Flow exporters were implemented and installed in a longer time than planned, and there were no templates for some models of routers.
